124881.explanation: The new council must consist of capable administrators. A balanced male-female ratio is desirable, but quality and suitability must always come first.

124883.explanation: Through the right to challenge, residents or local initiatives can take over municipal tasks, such as maintaining parks. This provides involved residents the opportunity to care for and improve their living environment themselves.

124885.explanation: Farm businesses may conduct secondary activities, such as selling dairy products. If notification or a permit is required for this, it must be requested in accordance with the applicable rules.

124886.explanation: We believe that generating sustainable energy is a responsibility of the residents themselves. They can use national subsidies where possible. When the national government provides subsidies via the municipality, the municipality ensures implementation.

124887.explanation: We are not against new distribution centers, but a careful consideration is needed for each location. The size of the distribution center and its impact on the surroundings must be taken into account.

124890.explanation: Municipal Interests wants less regulatory burden. A ban on open fireplaces and wood stoves does not fit with this and does not contribute to reducing regulations for residents.

124893.explanation: A small fee for bulky waste collection is desirable. There are options to bring bulky waste to the separation depot free of charge and to borrow an electric cargo bike there if needed.

124895.explanation: Entrepreneurs must make efforts themselves to make their buildings accessible for people with disabilities. Adaptations in public spaces for better accessibility are, of course, the municipality's responsibility.

124897.explanation: There will be changes in the coming years for local broadcasters. Currently, local broadcasters are supported by the national government. We await new legislation and how local (regional) broadcasters will be supported.

124899.explanation: Music lessons (as well as membership of a sports club, scouting, dance lessons, etc.) must be possible for every child. If parents lack sufficient financial means, they should be able to apply for support from the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture.

124900.explanation: Sports clubs must, in principle, manage their finances independently. If they get into financial trouble, the municipality can consider whether customized solutions can be applied.

124903.explanation: Camera surveillance should only be used where it is really necessary. Due to privacy concerns, strict rules apply. Therefore, a careful assessment is needed to determine if camera surveillance is the correct and most appropriate measure.

124908.explanation: Space for housing is scarce. Initiatives near municipal boundaries offer opportunities but require careful consideration: does it fit spatially, align with facilities and living environment, and does it enhance the quality of our villages?

124909.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits should not be forced into volunteer work. Volunteering is valuable when it arises from motivation and engagement.

124911.explanation: During the last council period, the income threshold was raised to 130% of the social assistance level. We want to maintain this 130% threshold for minimum income arrangements. This way, residents with low incomes just above social assistance still receive support and we prevent them from falling through the cracks.

124913.explanation: We believe parking spaces for electric charging should not be designated by the municipality. Charging points should arise at locations where there is actual demand so that they are used efficiently and meet residents' needs.

124914.explanation: We do not want a general 30 km/h zone throughout the entire built-up area. For through roads, 50 km/h remains a logical speed as long as it is safe, so accessibility and traffic flow are maintained.

124915.explanation: Care primarily concerns quality, accessibility, and availability. In a tight care market, choice is not always feasible and should not place extra pressure on caregivers or care organization.

124917.explanation: We want to keep the property tax (OZB) as low as possible while also meeting social challenges adequately. This means we must be able to invest in facilities and projects necessary for a livable, future-proof municipality.

124922.explanation: We house status holders according to the national tasking and thereby take our responsibility within existing agreements and rules.

124924.explanation: We want to allow solar panels on agricultural land, provided this is combined with the creation of nature and recreation, so that multiple goals are met and the landscape is enhanced.

124925.explanation: Community centers are important meeting places in the neighborhood for us. Community center Ponderosa is not owned by the municipality. Therefore, responsibility and initiative for renewal lie with the owner and not the municipality.

124929.explanation: We want to maintain 30% social rental housing in new construction but not increase it further. This way we preserve a balanced housing mix that contributes to a varied supply and good circulation in the housing market.

124930.explanation: We do not want to allow landlords to rent out individual rooms. The principle must be that tenants can have a home/apartment with private sanitary facilities and a kitchen.

124933.explanation: For housing construction, we want to make better use of available space and are also open to building upwards. With high-rise, a careful assessment is needed to determine if it fits the surroundings and which height is appropriate.

124934.explanation: Illegal boats causing nuisance require action. At the same time, we understand that boat dwellers often have no other place to live. Therefore, we advocate a careful and tactful approach, with regard for both livability and the human situation.

124935.explanation: We want to keep the waste pass. This prevents illegal dumping by people from outside the municipality and businesses. A fixed amount with a small fee per disposal encourages waste separation, reuse of raw materials, and keeps the costs of waste processing manageable.

124937.explanation: The same rules should apply to the hospitality industry on Julianalaan as to the rest of the hospitality sector in the municipality. It is important to actively work on preventing and limiting nuisance for local residents.

124881.explanation: DUS! believes that the right person should be in the right position, regardless of gender.

124883.explanation: According to DUS!, the greenery in the municipality should be well maintained. This can be done by the municipality, a landscaping company, or the resident - as long as it is done well, safely, and with care.

124885.explanation: DUS! supports offering alternative activities when farmers stop farming. However, DUS! wants to carefully consider when farmers want to run additional activities alongside their farm business.

124886.explanation: DUS! is strongly in favor of subsidies for residents who want to jointly implement sustainability measures.

124887.explanation: When the need arises, there should be room to establish more distribution centers in a sustainable, green, and clean manner.

124890.explanation: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves can cause (odor) nuisance and also lead to health problems for residents. DUS! prefers to see the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves decrease.

124893.explanation: Bulk waste collection at home should be free, as long as the costs for all residents do not increase as a result.

124895.explanation: DUS! wants every resident to have an accessible municipality and believes the municipality must invest in making (business) buildings more accessible.

124899.explanation: Stimulating art and cultural activities (including music) should start as early as possible, according to DUS!.

124900.explanation: Good sports facilities are important and DUS! believes the municipality has a major and important role in this.

124903.explanation: According to DUS!, camera surveillance does not lead to a safer municipality but to a feeling of false security.

124908.explanation: DUS! opposes building in the peat meadow area and valuable green spaces.

124909.explanation: Doing (volunteer) work should not be forced as a condition for social assistance benefits. DUS! does not believe in this quid pro quo approach.

124911.explanation: DUS! believes the municipality should help all residents who need assistance.

124913.explanation: DUS! supports electric cars but prefers even more investment in cycling, walking, and public transport.

124914.explanation: DUS! believes that lowering the maximum speed will increase traffic safety.

124915.explanation: Residents should have the freedom to choose care from a provider that suits them.

124917.explanation: DUS! prefers the lowest possible charges for residents without compromising on facilities.

124922.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit should, according to DUS!, receive the same opportunities as Dutch residents.

124924.explanation: DUS! wants to see more solar panels in the municipality-not only on agricultural land but also on large roofs, small roofs, and in gardens.

124925.explanation: DUS! would like the municipality to invest in local community centers and neighborhood rooms if needed.

124929.explanation: DUS! wants to invest in more social rental housing, but not more than the norm of 30%. More social rental homes would come at the expense of other types of housing (such as mid-range rental) where shortages also exist.

124930.explanation: DUS! supports affordable shared housing (hospitaverhuur), but within set frameworks and specific conditions.

124933.explanation: DUS! acknowledges that high-rise buildings are sometimes unavoidable, but this must be considered per area. It should remain in keeping with the village character of Uithoorn and De Kwakel.

124934.explanation: Illegally occupied boats lead to clutter and an increased feeling of insecurity. DUS! wants these boats removed immediately.

124935.explanation: The new system with the waste pass encourages residents to separate waste more. DUS! is a big supporter of this system.

124937.explanation: Pubs on Koningin Julianalaan may no longer admit new guests after a certain time to limit nuisance for local residents.

124881.explanation: VVD opposes a mandatory equal male-female ratio in the executive board. We first choose quality, experience, and connection with our villages. Diversity is important, but we do not want to enforce fixed quotas.

124883.explanation: If residents want to manage greenery or a playground themselves, the municipality will help. VVD wants agreements about, for example, quality and safety.

124885.explanation: Entrepreneurship is important, but it must fit the plan and not cause inconvenience to neighbors or traffic. VVD chooses balance.

124886.explanation: If residents want to generate sustainable energy together, VVD welcomes this, but they must arrange their own financing.

124887.explanation: We do not consider new large industrial halls on the outskirts a good direction. VVD will only consider this in very exceptional cases, with strict conditions: demonstrable necessity, appropriate location, little extra traffic, no nuisance, and clear agreements about landscape and appearance.

124890.explanation: VVD does not want a general ban on burning wood. However: burning must be without nuisance, and in case of complaints, rules, inspections, and interventions apply.

124893.explanation: Taking bulk waste to the waste facility is the individual's responsibility. VVD ensures the recycling center is easily accessible and tackles dumping firmly.

124895.explanation: Accessibility is part of normal business operations. The municipality helps with clear rules and practical advice, not with extra obligations.

124897.explanation: Extra funding is not necessary. A local broadcaster mainly has a basic role as a local emergency broadcaster and information channel. VVD wants this arranged well and modestly, without structural extra subsidies.

124899.explanation: Culture is valuable, but not every child should automatically receive subsidies. VVD focuses support on families that need it.

124900.explanation: Associations are important. Temporary support can be given, but only with a good plan and clear agreements, says VVD.

124903.explanation: Cameras can help at real hotspots. VVD wants targeted deployment, clear explanations, and strict privacy rules.

124908.explanation: Housing shortage requires speed. VVD believes building on the outskirts is sometimes necessary, with greenery, parking, and accessibility well arranged.

124909.explanation: Participation is the goal. VVD thinks a reciprocal obligation can help guide people toward work and routine, with customization where needed.

124911.explanation: Help should go to those who are truly stuck. VVD improves the targeted approach, not by broadly expanding the criteria.

124913.explanation: VVD wants charging to be possible. We place charging stations where there is demand and pay attention to parking pressure and the power grid.

124914.explanation: VVD does not want a fixed 30 km/h everywhere. 30 km/h near schools and residential streets; main roads remain smooth and safe.

124915.explanation: Choice in care is important. VVD prioritizes quality and safety.

124917.explanation: As far as VVD is concerned, the recent property tax increase approved by a majority of the Council is unnecessary. VVD wants to keep property tax low and predictable. No extra increase; reduction only if the budget allows.

124922.explanation: VVD wants to give priority to residents with a verifiable local connection when allocating homes where possible.

124924.explanation: VVD wants solar panels on roofs and business parks first. Agricultural land is only used in exceptional cases, in urgent need and with good integration into the landscape. This way we strongly limit it, without ruling it out completely.

124925.explanation: VVD believes renewal is not a municipal duty if we are not the owner. First better utilize what is already in the neighborhood and only invest when there is demonstrable added value.

124929.explanation: A fixed percentage of affordable housing does not always fit. VVD builds according to need, with rental and ownership, and with livability in order.

124930.explanation: Room rentals can help with the housing shortage. VVD only wants this under strict conditions and not in every street or neighborhood. Nuisance, parking pressure, and safety must be prevented, with clear rules and strict enforcement.

124933.explanation: High-rise buildings can be in the right place. VVD demands quality, good integration, and solutions for parking and traffic.

124934.explanation: Illegal situations must stop quickly. VVD wants strict enforcement within the law so that nuisance does not persist.

124935.explanation: Paying per waste pass does not work well. VVD wants a simple and fair system and less dumping.

124937.explanation: VVD opposes a general rule after 23:00. We choose customization per permit: clear agreements on opening hours and tackling nuisance. This way we protect residents and keep a lively center.

124881.explanation: It is about suitability and availability of candidates, not gender. When suitability and availability are equal, proportional representation of women and men can be aimed for.

124883.explanation: CDA distinguishes between park management and the management of utilities. Park management can- with good agreements-be transferred to residents. For management of, for example, a playground, safety aspects must be strictly observed.

124885.explanation: This depends on the circumstances and should be decided on an individual basis.

124886.explanation: In principle, CDA agrees with this as long as this support is needed; however, it should not become a profit model.

124887.explanation: It depends on circumstances such as the zoning plan and the need for other uses like housing. Uithoorn borders Aalsmeer and Schiphol where many distribution centers already exist, so there is less necessity to locate these in Uithoorn.

124890.explanation: As cozy as fireplaces and wood stoves may be, the consequences for the environment and people are too detrimental due to the release of fine dust. Note: This pollution is often greater and more harmful than road traffic.

124893.explanation: Nothing is truly free. The costs of waste disposal must be funded somehow. Residents can also take measures themselves, such as borrowing a cargo bike through the municipality. In exceptional cases, customization can be applied.

124895.explanation: This is primarily the entrepreneur's own task. In some cases, subsidies may be possible.

124897.explanation: The local broadcaster (Radio Rick) receives a legally established contribution based on the number of households. Additionally, there are revenues from advertisements and sponsorships. If the local broadcaster is used for municipal purposes, it must be paid for this.

124899.explanation: CDA is a strong supporter of music education. Parents are asked for a contribution based on their ability to pay. Parents who cannot afford it are eligible for a subsidy.

124900.explanation: In principle, disagree but there are exceptions. The municipality already pays a lot for the grounds and their maintenance. Additionally, the municipality has the Youth Fund Sports & Culture and Adult Fund for low-income families.

124903.explanation: Maintain existing camera surveillance at known nuisance locations. Expand quickly if new nuisance spots arise.

124908.explanation: We assume this refers to the built-up area. Each case must be assessed whether housing outside this area is possible. There must also be room left for nature and cultural landscapes.

124909.explanation: CDA favors joint responsibility, so a reciprocal obligation may be required where possible. This also promotes participation in society. We don't write anyone off but want everyone to participate and count.

124911.explanation: CDA wants to actively keep residents out of severe poverty. The current limit of 130 percent of the social minimum is not always appropriate. There must be room to provide customization in that case.

124913.explanation: CDA welcomes the use of electric vehicles, but charging costs must be cost-covering, so no profit model.

124914.explanation: 50 km/h where traffic safety allows it for through traffic. For residential areas and roundabouts, 30 km/h is the maximum speed for all road users, as is currently set in Uithoorn/De Kwakel. Safety takes precedence over speed.

124915.explanation: Care providers must be neutral; no distinction may be made based on religion, belief, gender, etc. The same applies to those receiving care.

124917.explanation: CDA wants the property tax to be lowered. That was also the reason CDA opposed the coalition parties' proposal to structurally increase it by 2 percent annually on top of the indexation.

124922.explanation: Priority rules must be set so that different groups have equal chances of allocation.

124924.explanation: Our territory is small, so we prefer solar panels on roofs so that farmland remains available for agriculture and other purposes.

124925.explanation: Neighborhood centers play a crucial role in connection, information provision, assistance, organizing activities at neighborhood level, and are the eyes and ears of the community. In emergencies, they fulfill a central role. IF this also applies to Ponderosa, the municipality must invest in it.

124929.explanation: If necessary and possible, we want to go to 35%.

124930.explanation: Under strict conditions set by the municipality for reasonable rental prices and maintenance. This is solely due to the housing shortage. Room rental with a landlady is seen by the CDA as one of the solutions to the housing shortage.

124933.explanation: But not higher than the spire of the Schanskerk at 35 meters.

124935.explanation: We support waste separation but see more potential in sorting afterwards. The current system with tariff differentiation has side effects due to locked containers. We expect better results from sorting afterwards in the future.

124937.explanation: The existing rule to stop admitting new guests after 1:00 a.m. must be maintained. Naturally, the operator remains responsible for minimizing nuisance caused by guests to residents. Permit requirements must be established and enforced.

124881.explanation: The Uithoorn council currently consists only of men. It would be good if women were included in the coming period.

124883.explanation: That can be done with good agreements. The municipality remains responsible for quality and continuity.

124885.explanation: That must be assessed when an application is submitted.

124886.explanation: The municipality can support an energy collective.

124887.explanation: More distribution centers are not appropriate and not desirable in our municipality.

124890.explanation: Wood smoke is harmful to the environment and bad for health. Especially during calm and foggy weather, burning should not take place.

124893.explanation: It is reasonable to ask for a contribution for collection. Bringing waste yourself remains free.

124899.explanation: Music lessons are important for every child. Subsidies can be provided through the Sports and Culture fund or other funds.

124900.explanation: In principle yes, but that depends on the situation.

124903.explanation: Only in places where it is really necessary and meaningful.

124908.explanation: The peat meadow area must remain open.

124909.explanation: It is good that people on social assistance remain active. Unfortunately, that is not always possible. Obligations in exchange for benefits are undesirable.

124911.explanation: The limit is currently set at 130% of the minimum. Compared to other municipalities, this is relatively high. Therefore, the threshold does not need to be raised now. However, the municipality can still do more to reach people who need help.

124914.explanation: Through roads may be exempted where appropriate.

124915.explanation: Care must be of good quality, independent, and accessible to everyone.

124917.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is not too high.

124922.explanation: The municipality must accommodate these status holders. A priority scheme helps with this. The large shortage of social rental housing is not due to refugees and must be solved by building many more social rental homes.

124924.explanation: Solar panels on roofs and for example parking lots are much better than on agricultural land.

124925.explanation: Ponderosa is owned by a foundation. The municipality can possibly support renewal.

124929.explanation: The housing shortage is greatest among people with low and middle incomes. Therefore, many more social rental homes must be built. The share of affordable housing must increase significantly.

124930.explanation: Room rental by for example a landlady and forms of shared housing should become more possible. Splitting properties into multiple rental rooms causes nuisance and unscrupulous renting.

124933.explanation: Apartments up to approximately 6 floors can be built, but no high-rise buildings.

124934.explanation: Illegal living is prohibited but is a consequence of the housing shortage. Sometimes municipal assistance is needed.

124935.explanation: No. Municipalities are instructed by the national government to reduce residual waste. This can be done by better separation. The waste pass can help with this.

124937.explanation: Nighttime bars cause a lot of nuisance.

statements.title: High-rise buildings should be built on Amstelplein.

titleParts.glossary: Buildings with homes or businesses on multiple floors, for example apartments or flats.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed in Uithoorn.

titleParts.text: In Uithoorn moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a playground or green area from the municipality.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Uithoorn.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the waste-pass system.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should have illegally occupied boats towed away immediately.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Uithoorn should renovate Community Centre Ponderosa.
At least 35% of the new homes to be built in Uithoorn should be social rental housing. Currently, 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Uithoorn moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: Cafés on Koningin Julianalaan may not admit new customers after 11:00 PM.
